paper_title: Late Eocene–early Miocene evolution of the southern Australian subtropical front: a marine palynological approach

methods.description: This method describes the process of preparing and analyzing sedimentary samples for palynological content to study the evolution of the southern Australian subtropical front.

steps.description: Dried sediment samples were crushed and weighed (on average 10 g, standard deviation, SD, of <1 g).
steps.searchable_summary: Crush and weigh dried sediment samples.

steps.description: Samples were digested with 30% cold hydrochloric acid (HCl) and 38% cold hydrofluoric acid (HF) for carbonate and silicate removal respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Digest samples with HCl and HF to remove carbonates and silicates.
outputs: samples with removed carbonates and silicates

steps.description: The remaining palynological residues were sieved on a 10 µm nylon mesh, using an ultrasonic bath to disintegrate agglutinated organic particles.
steps.searchable_summary: Sieve residues on a 10 µm mesh and use ultrasonic bath for disintegration.
inputs: samples with removed carbonates and silicates

steps.description: The palynological residues were mounted on glass slides using glycerine jelly, sealed with nail varnish.
steps.searchable_summary: Mount residues on glass slides with glycerine jelly and seal with nail varnish.

steps.description: Counted (under 400× magnification) using a Leica DM2500 LED transmitted light optical microscope. When possible, at least 200 dinocyst specimens were counted.
Slides containing less than 50 dinocyst specimens were excluded from further analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Exclude slides with less than 50 dinocyst specimens.
